rulings.Case Title: ETHAN CHAN VS. HOWARD J. LEWIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANT HOWARD LEWIS' MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Prayer for treble damages is stricken without leave to amend. References to Defendants as "Lewis, as Trustee" and "Maybon Trust" are stricken with leave to amend to consistently refer to defendant as "Lewis, as Trustee." Motion is denied as to allegations listed under section 2 in the Notice of Motion. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE KRAJEWSKI HARBERT VS. YEVA INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgmentorder And To Reopen Case; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Plaintiff; Declaration Of Gary Saunders, Esq.; Declaration Of Romel Ambarchyan
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2017 line 4. PLAINTIFF ANNE HARBERT's Motion To Set Aside Judgment order And To Reopen Case DENIED. Plaintiff moves for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b). Per Huh v. Wang (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 1406, Plaintiff must provide an explanation for the delay in seeking the relief. Plaintiff admits in her declaration that she knew about the Demurrer order as on May 1, 2017 (Declaration, paragraph 9). Plaintiff failed to seek relief until June 12, 2017. Plaintiff "offered no evidence explaining the delay in seeking relief. Given the absolute failure of proof on this point, there is no basis for granting relief." Huh at 1422. Estate of Bear (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 753 cited by Plaintiff is not on point. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHIO KUAN AO-IEONG ET AL VS. GUO KING CHEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2017 line 5. DEFENDANT GUO CHEN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts re: Defendant's conscious disregard of Plaintiffs health and safety, being on notice of the issues and refusing to address them. Whether these allegations are true in light of the facts that cannot be properly considered at this stage of the litigation, is an issue for another motion/trial. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. CHU CHU'S GOODS INC. DBA HARVEST HILLS MARKET
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 20, 2017 line 6. DEFENDANT CHU CHU'S GOODS, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burned and show that the complaint is not drawn in conformity with the laws. Defendant and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. Failure to do so may result in the denial of the motion. = (501/REQ) late 3:19
